schemas.name: Temporary GST — Casual Taxable Person
schemas.description: Temporary GST registration for casual taxable persons. Exhibitions, trade fairs, seasonal business. Advance tax deposit. 90-day validity. Virtual Auditor.

schemas.name: Temporary GST Registration
schemas.description: Temporary GST registration for casual taxable persons. Exhibitions, trade fairs, seasonal business. Advance tax deposit. 90-day validity. Virtual Auditor.

schemas.name: Temporary GST Registration
schemas.description: Temporary GST registration for casual taxable persons. Exhibitions, trade fairs, seasonal business. Advance tax deposit. 90-day validity. Virtual Auditor.

step.text: Determine if casual taxable person provisions apply
step.name: Step 1

step.text: Apply for temporary registration on GST portal
step.name: Step 2

mainEntity.name: Who is a casual taxable person?

acceptedAnswer.text: A person who occasionally supplies goods/services in a state where they have no fixed place of business. Example: a Delhi-based manufacturer exhibiting at a Chennai trade fair.

mainEntity.name: How far in advance must I apply?

acceptedAnswer.text: At least 5 days before commencing business in the state. Registration valid for 90 days from the effective date.

Person who occasionally undertakes taxable transactions in a state where they have no fixed place of business. Example: exhibitor at trade fair in another state. Must obtain temporary GST registration before commencing supply.

mainEntity.name: How long is temporary GST valid?

acceptedAnswer.text: 90 days from date of registration. Can be extended for additional 90 days by applying before expiry. Must file returns and surrender registration after temporary period ends.
Yes. Must deposit estimated tax liability for the entire registration period in advance at the time of registration. Deposit adjusted against actual liability in returns.

mainEntity.name: Can temporary GST claim input tax credit?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes. Casual taxable persons can claim ITC on inputs and input services used for making taxable supplies during the temporary registration period. Must be claimed in returns filed during that period.

mainEntity.name: What happens after temporary period ends?

acceptedAnswer.text: File all pending returns. Any excess tax deposited: claim refund. Surrender temporary GSTIN. If business becomes permanent in that state: apply for regular registration.
